mission.title: istio: Multi cluster DNS breaks due to they way iptables and conntrack operates
mission.description: Multi cluster DNS breaks due to they way iptables and conntrack operates. Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current istio deployment
steps.description:
Verify your istio version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
helm list -n istio 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working istio installation.

steps.title: Review istio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get configmap -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio
```
(**NOTE**: This is used to report product bugs:
  To report a security vulnerability, please visit <https://istio.io/about/security-vulnerabilities>
  To ask questions about how to use Istio, please visit <https://discuss.istio.io>)

**Bug

steps.title: Apply the fix for Multi cluster DNS breaks due to they way iptables and…
steps.description:
iptables redirection is essentially "cached" by conntrack. This works ok
for TCP, but for UDP this means we are not routing on a per-packet basis
like we require for the DNS proxy. See
https://serverfault.com/questions/741104/iptables-redirect-works-only-for-first-packet/741108#741108.

This means
```yaml
udp      17 83 src=100.96.126.31 dst=169.254.20.10 sport=51692 dport=53 src=169.254.20.10 dst=100.96.126.31 sport=53 dport=51692 [ASSURED] mark=0 use=1
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get events -n istio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Multi cluster DNS breaks due to they way iptables and…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
iptables redirection is essentially "cached" by conntrack. This works ok
for TCP, but for UDP this means we are not routing on a per-packet basis
like we require for the DNS proxy. See
https://serverfault.com/questions/741104/iptables-redirect-works-only-for-first-packet/741108#741108.

control plane version: 1.9.5
data plane version: 1.9.5 (191 proxies)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
